notes: 30 questions for RAGAS faithfulness + context-relevance + answer-relevance. No reference answers — RAGAS judges against retrieved chunks, not a ground truth string. Categories tag the failure mode each question is designed to expose.

questions.question: What is the default value of the --max-pods flag on the kubelet?
What HTTP status code does the API server return when an admission webhook times out?
Which field on a Service resource is used to select the Pods that traffic is routed to?
What kubectl command creates a PodDisruptionBudget from a YAML file?
What is the default value of the --leader-elect flag on kube-scheduler?
What is the name of the controller that handles graceful, ordered scaling of replicas with persistent identities?
What kind of object does Kubernetes use to expose a Service to traffic outside the cluster via HTTP/HTTPS routing rules?
What does the spec.minAvailable field on a PodDisruptionBudget specify?
Which kube-controller-manager flag controls the period at which it checks node health?
What is the default cluster DNS suffix used inside Pods?
How does an Ingress controller decide which backend Pod to forward a request to?
What is the difference between a ReplicaSet and a Deployment, and when should you use each?
How are ConfigMaps and Secrets exposed to a running container, and what are the security trade-offs?
What is the difference between a liveness probe, a readiness probe, and a startup probe?
How do taints and tolerations interact with node affinity rules during scheduling?
What is the difference between voluntary and involuntary disruptions in Kubernetes?
How does a StatefulSet provide stable network identities to its Pods?
What happens to a Pod's PersistentVolumeClaim when the Pod is deleted vs. when the StatefulSet is deleted?
How does the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler decide when to scale up, and what metrics can it use?
What is the difference between a DaemonSet and a Deployment with one replica per node?
If I want zero-downtime rolling updates for a StatefulSet that uses persistent storage, what features must I configure together?
What happens when a Node becomes NotReady — how does Kubernetes decide when to evict its Pods, and what controls the timing?
How do I run a workload that must execute exactly once per node, survive node restarts, and be drained gracefully during maintenance?
What is the full path a request takes from an external client to a specific Pod when the cluster uses an Ingress, a Service of type ClusterIP, and a Deployment?
How can I prevent a noisy neighbour Pod from consuming all the memory on a node, and what happens if the limit is exceeded?
What is the interaction between PodDisruptionBudgets and node drain operations during a cluster upgrade?
How does the scheduler decide which node to place a Pod on when there are taints, node affinity rules, topology spread constraints, and resource requests in play?
If a Service of type LoadBalancer is created in a cluster running on a cloud provider, what sequence of components are involved in provisioning the external load balancer?
How can I dynamically provision storage for a StatefulSet such that each replica gets its own PersistentVolume, and what controls whether the volume survives Pod deletion?
What is the full sequence of events when a Pod fails its liveness probe — what does the kubelet do, what happens to the container, and how does the Deployment controller respond?
